    20	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    21	>    - Auto: `spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml`
    22	>    - Confirm: `spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml`
    23	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    24	>
    25	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    26	
    27	## CONSTRAINTS
    28	
    29	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    30	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    31	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    32	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    33	  - `review_target`, `review_target_type`, `review_dimensions`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    34	
    35	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    36	
    37	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    38	
    39	---
    40	
    41	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    42	
    43	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: This prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    44	
    45	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
    46	
    47	```
    48	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
    49	
    50	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
    51	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
    52	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
    53	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
    54	
    55	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
    56	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
    57	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
    58	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
    59	
    60	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
    61	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
    62	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
    63	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
    64	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10
    65	
    66	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
    67	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
    68	
    69	5. Search for prior work (background):
    70	   - memory_context({ input: review_target OR "deep-review", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
    71	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
    72	
    73	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
    74	
    75	   Q0. Review Target (if not in command): What to review?
    76	     Examples: spec folder path, `skill:sk-name`, `agent:name`, `track:NN--name`, or file paths/globs
    77	
    78	   Q1_type. Review Target Type (auto-detect from Q0, confirm):
    79	     A) spec-folder -- Review spec artifacts + implementation files
    80	     B) skill -- SKILL.md + references/ + assets/ + agents + commands
    81	     C) agent -- Agent family across all runtimes
    82	     D) track -- All child spec folders in a feature track
    83	     E) files -- Arbitrary file paths or globs
    84	
    85	   Q_dims. Which review dimensions? (default: all 4)
    86	     All: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
    87	     Or specify a subset (e.g., "correctness, security")
    88	
    89	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
    90	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
    91	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
    92	     C) Update related [if match found]
    93	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
    94	
    95	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
    96	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval
    97	     B) Interactive -- pause at each iteration for review
    98	
    99	   Q3. Max Iterations (if not set via flag):
   100	     Default is 7 for review. Change? [Enter number or press enter for default]
   101	
   102	   Reply format: "skill:sk-deep-research, B, all, A, A" or "A, correctness security, B, B, 5"
   103	
   104	7. WAIT for user response (DO NOT PROCEED)
   105	
   106	8. Parse response and store ALL results:
   107	   - review_target = [from Q0 or $ARGUMENTS]
   108	   - review_target_type = [from Q1_type, auto-detected]
   109	   - review_dimensions = [from Q_dims or default "all"]
   110	   - spec_choice = [A/B/C/D from Q1]
   111	   - spec_path = [derived path]
   112	   - execution_mode = [AUTONOMOUS/INTERACTIVE]
   113	   - maxIterations = [from Q3 or flag or default 7]
   114	   - convergenceThreshold = [from flag or default 0.10]
   115	
   116	9. SET STATUS: PASSED
   117	
   118	STOP HERE - Wait for user answers before continuing.
   119	
   120	DO NOT proceed until user explicitly answers
   121	NEVER auto-create spec folders without confirmation
   122	NEVER split questions into multiple prompts
   123	```
   124	
   125	**Phase Output:**
   126	
   127	- `review_target` | `review_target_type` | `review_dimensions`
   128	- `spec_choice` | `spec_path` | `execution_mode` | `maxIterations` | `convergenceThreshold`
   129	
   130	---
   131	
   132	# Deep Review
   133	
   134	Conduct autonomous iterative code review with convergence detection. Each iteration dispatches a fresh LEAF agent (`@deep-review`) that reads externalized state, performs focused review work across configured dimensions, and writes findings to files.
   135	
   136	```yaml
   137	role: Deep Review Loop Manager
   138	purpose: Run iterative review cycles until convergence or max iterations
   139	action: Execute YAML workflow managing init, loop, synthesis, and save phases
   140	operating_mode:
   141	  workflow: iterative_loop
   142	  compliance: MANDATORY
   143	  execution: autonomous_or_interactive
   144	  validation: convergence_detection
   145	```
   146	
   147	---
   148	
   149	## 1. PURPOSE
   150	
   151	Run an iterative loop for code review: Initialize the review packet under `{spec_folder}/review/`, dispatch `@deep-review` agent per iteration, evaluate convergence across review dimensions, and synthesize findings into `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md`. Use when auditing code, specs, skills, agents, or tracks for quality and release readiness.
   152	
   153	---
   154	
   155	## 2. CONTRACT
   156	
   157	**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` -- Review target with optional flags and mode suffix
   158	**Outputs:** Spec folder with `{spec_folder}/review/` packet + state files + `STATUS=<OK|FAIL|CANCELLED>`
   159	
   160	---
   161	
   162	## 3. WORKFLOW OVERVIEW
   163	
   164	| Phase | Name | Purpose | Outputs |
   165	|-------|------|---------|---------|
   166	| Init | Initialize | Scope discovery, resolve files, create config + strategy with review dimensions | Review packet in `{spec_folder}/review/` |
   167	| Loop | Iterate | Dispatch @deep-review agent per dimension, evaluate review convergence + quality guards | `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` files, `review/deep-review-dashboard.md` |
   168	| Synth | Synthesize | Build finding registry, deduplicate, compile `review/review-report.md` | `review/review-report.md` (9 sections) |
   169	| Save | Preserve | Refresh continuity support artifact | support artifact generated via `generate-context.js` |
   170	
   171	### Execution Modes
   172	
   173	| Mode | Invocation | Behavior |
   174	|------|-----------|----------|
   175	| `:auto` | `/spec_kit:deep-review:auto "target"` | All iterations without approval |
   176	| `:confirm` | `/spec_kit:deep-review:confirm "target"` | Multi-gate review at setup, iteration, and synthesis |
   177	| (default) | `/spec_kit:deep-review "target"` | Ask user to choose mode during setup |
   178	
   179	---
   180	
   181	## 4. KEY BEHAVIORS
   182	
   183	### Autonomous Mode (`:auto`)
   184	
   185	- Executes all iterations without user approval gates
   186	- Self-validates at each convergence checkpoint
   187	- Makes informed decisions on dimension ordering and iteration focus
   188	- Documents all significant decisions in `review/deep-review-dashboard.md`
   189	- Stops when convergence threshold is met or max iterations reached
   190	
   191	### Interactive Mode (`:confirm`)
   192	
   193	- Pauses after each iteration for user approval
   194	- Presents iteration findings summary with dimension coverage
   195	- Options at each gate: Approve, Review Details, Modify Scope, Skip Dimension, Abort
   196	- Documents user decisions at each checkpoint
   197	- Allows course correction of dimensions, depth, or scope throughout
   198	
   199	---
   200	
   201	## 5. INSTRUCTIONS
   202	
   203	After setup phase passes, load and execute the appropriate YAML prompt based on execution_mode:
   204	
   205	- **AUTONOMOUS**: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml`
   206	- **INTERACTIVE**: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml`
   207	
   208	The YAML contains the full loop workflow: initialization, iteration dispatch, convergence detection, synthesis, and memory save.
   209	
   210	---
   211	
   212	## 6. OUTPUT FORMATS
   213	
   214	**Review Success:**
   215	```
   216	Deep review complete.
   217	Iterations: [N] | Stop reason: [converged|max_iterations|all_dimensions_clean]
   218	Findings: P0=[N] P1=[N] P2=[N] | Verdict: [PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL] [PASS may include hasAdvisories=true]
   219	Artifacts: review/review-report.md, [N] iteration files in review/, continuity support artifact refreshed
   220	Ready for: /spec_kit:plan [remediation] (if FAIL/CONDITIONAL)
   221	STATUS=OK PATH=[spec-folder-path]
   222	```
   223	
   224	**Failure:**
   225	```
   226	Error: [error description]  Phase: [phase name]
   227	STATUS=FAIL ERROR="[message]"
   228	```
   229	
   230	---
   231	
   232	## 7. MEMORY INTEGRATION
   233	
   234	### Before Starting
   235	- `memory_context({ input: review_target, intent: "understand" })` -- Load prior review context
   236	- Inject results into strategy.md "Known Context" section
   237	
   238	### Code Context Bootstrap
   239	- Use CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`) to find relevant code examples before starting review
   240	- Query: 2-5 word concept descriptions related to the review target
   241	- Inject discovered code patterns into strategy.md "Known Context" section alongside memory findings
   242	
   243	### After Completing
   244	- `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data.json [spec-folder]`
   245	- Verify that `{spec_folder}/memory/` contains the generated memory artifact
   246	
   247	### Anchor Tags (Automatic)
   248	`ANCHOR:deep-review-[target]`, `ANCHOR:findings`, `ANCHOR:convergence-report`
   249	
   250	---
   251	
   252	## 8. SKILL REFERENCE
   253	
   254	Full protocol documentation: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md`
   255	
   256	Key references:
   257	- Review contract: `sk-deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml`
   258	- Review strategy template: `sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md`
   259	- Review dashboard template: `sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_dashboard.md`
   260	- Review config template: `sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json`

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	description: Autonomous deep research loop - iterative investigation with convergence detection. Supports :auto and :confirm modes
     3	argument-hint: "<topic> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N]"
     4	allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, WebFetch, memory_context, memory_search, mcp__cocoindex_code__search, code_graph_query, code_graph_context
     5	---
     6	
     7	> **EXECUTION PROTOCOL -- READ FIRST**
     8	>
     9	> This command runs a structured YAML workflow. Do NOT dispatch agents from this document.
    10	>
    11	> **YOUR FIRST ACTION:**
    12	> 1. Run the unified setup phase in this Markdown entrypoint and resolve:
    13	>    - `research_topic`
    14	>    - `spec_folder`
    15	>    - `execution_mode`
    16	>    - `maxIterations`
    17	>    - `convergenceThreshold`
    18	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    19	>    - Auto: `spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml`
    20	>    - Confirm: `spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml`
    21	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    22	>
    23	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    24	
    25	## CONSTRAINTS
    26	
    27	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    28	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    29	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    30	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    31	  - `research_topic`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    32	
    33	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    34	
    35	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    36	
    37	---
    38	
    39	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    40	
    41	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: This prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    42	
    43	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
    44	
    45	```
    46	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
    47	
    48	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
    49	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
    50	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
    51	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
    52	
    53	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
    54	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
    55	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
    56	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
    57	
    58	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
    59	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
    60	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
    61	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
    62	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05
    63	
    64	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
    65	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
    66	
    67	5. Search for prior work (background):
    68	   - memory_context({ input: research_topic OR "deep-research", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
    69	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
    70	
    71	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
    72	
    73	   Q0. Research Topic (if not in command): What topic to research deeply?
    74	
    75	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
    76	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
    77	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
    78	     C) Update related [if match found]
    79	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
    80	
    81	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
    82	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval
    83	     B) Interactive -- pause at each iteration for review
    84	
    85	   Q3. Max Iterations (if not set via flag):
    86	     Default is 10. Change? [Enter number or press enter for default]
    87	
    88	   Reply format: "A, A" or "WebSocket research, A, B, 15"
    89	
    90	7. WAIT for user response (DO NOT PROCEED)
    91	
    92	8. Parse response and store ALL results:
    93	   - research_topic = [from Q0 or $ARGUMENTS]
    94	   - spec_choice = [A/B/C/D from Q1]
    95	   - spec_path = [derived path]
    96	   - execution_mode = [AUTONOMOUS/INTERACTIVE]
    97	   - maxIterations = [from Q3 or flag or default 10]
    98	   - convergenceThreshold = [from flag or default 0.05]
    99	
   100	9. SET STATUS: PASSED
   101	
   102	STOP HERE - Wait for user answers before continuing.
   103	
   104	DO NOT proceed until user explicitly answers
   105	NEVER auto-create spec folders without confirmation
   106	NEVER split questions into multiple prompts
   107	```
   108	
   109	**Phase Output:**
   110	- `research_topic` | `spec_choice` | `spec_path`
   111	- `execution_mode` | `maxIterations` | `convergenceThreshold`
   112	
   113	---
   114	
   115	# Deep Research
   116	
   117	Conduct autonomous iterative deep research with convergence detection. Each iteration dispatches a fresh LEAF agent (`@deep-research`) that reads externalized state, performs focused investigation, and writes findings to files.
   118	
   119	For code review and quality auditing, see `/spec_kit:deep-review`.
   120	
   121	```yaml
   122	role: Deep Research Loop Manager
   123	purpose: Run iterative research cycles until convergence or max iterations
   124	action: Execute YAML workflow managing init, loop, synthesis, and save phases
   125	operating_mode:
   126	  workflow: iterative_loop
   127	  compliance: MANDATORY
   128	  execution: autonomous_or_interactive
   129	  validation: convergence_detection
   130	```
   131	
   132	---
   133	
   134	## 1. PURPOSE
   135	
   136	Run an iterative loop for deep research: Initialize state, dispatch `@deep-research` agent per iteration, evaluate convergence, and synthesize findings into research/research.md. Use when deep investigation requiring multiple rounds of discovery.
   137	
   138	---
   139	
   140	## 2. CONTRACT
   141	
   142	**Inputs:** `$ARGUMENTS` -- Research topic with optional flags and mode suffix
   143	**Outputs:** Spec folder with research/research.md + state files + `STATUS=<OK|FAIL|CANCELLED>`
   144	
   145	---
   146	
   147	## 3. WORKFLOW OVERVIEW
   148	
   149	| Phase | Name | Purpose | Outputs |
   150	|-------|------|---------|---------|
   151	| Init | Initialize | Create config, strategy (with research charter), state files | State files in `research/` |
   152	| Loop | Iterate | Dispatch @deep-research agent, evaluate convergence + quality guards, generate dashboard | iteration-NNN.md files, deep-research-dashboard.md |
   153	| Synth | Synthesize | Compile final research/research.md | research/research.md (17 sections) |
   154	| Save | Preserve | Refresh continuity support artifact | support artifact generated via `generate-context.js` |
   155	
   156	### Execution Modes
   157	
   158	| Mode | Invocation | Behavior |
   159	|------|-----------|----------|
   160	| `:auto` | `/spec_kit:deep-research:auto "topic"` | All iterations without approval |
   161	| `:confirm` | `/spec_kit:deep-research:confirm "topic"` | Multi-gate review at setup, iteration, and synthesis |
   162	| (default) | `/spec_kit:deep-research "topic"` | Ask user to choose mode during setup |
   163	
   164	---
   165	
   166	## 4. INSTRUCTIONS
   167	
   168	After setup phase passes, load and execute the appropriate YAML prompt based on execution_mode:
   169	
   170	- **AUTONOMOUS**: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml`
   171	- **INTERACTIVE**: `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml`
   172	
   173	The YAML contains the full loop workflow: initialization, iteration dispatch, convergence detection, synthesis, and memory save.
   174	
   175	---
   176	
   177	## 5. OUTPUT FORMATS
   178	
   179	**Success:**
   180	```
   181	Deep research complete.
   182	Iterations: [N] | Stop reason: [converged|max_iterations|all_answered]
   183	Artifacts: research/research.md, [N] iteration files, continuity support artifact refreshed
   184	Ready for: /spec_kit:plan [feature-description]
   185	STATUS=OK PATH=[spec-folder-path]
   186	```
   187	
   188	**Failure:**
   189	```
   190	Error: [error description]  Phase: [phase name]
   191	STATUS=FAIL ERROR="[message]"
   192	```
   193	
   194	---
   195	
   196	## 6. MEMORY INTEGRATION
   197	
   198	### Before Starting
   199	- `memory_context({ input: topic, intent: "understand" })` -- Load prior research
   200	- Inject results into strategy.md "Known Context" section
   201	
   202	### Code Context Bootstrap
   203	- Use CocoIndex (`mcp__cocoindex_code__search`) to find relevant code examples before starting research
   204	- Query: 2-5 word concept descriptions related to the research topic
   205	- Inject discovered code patterns into strategy.md "Known Context" section alongside memory findings
   206	
   207	### After Completing
   208	- `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js /tmp/save-context-data.json [spec-folder]`
   209	- Verify that `{spec_folder}/memory/` contains the generated memory artifact
   210	
   211	### Anchor Tags (Automatic)
   212	`ANCHOR:deep-research-[topic]`, `ANCHOR:findings`, `ANCHOR:convergence-report`
   213	
   214	---
   215	
   216	## 7. SKILL REFERENCE
   217	
   218	Full protocol documentation: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md`
   219	
   220	Key references:
   221	- Loop protocol: `sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md`
   222	- State formats: `sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md`
   223	- Convergence: `sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md`
   224	- Quick reference: `sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md`
   225	
   226	For code review, see `sk-deep-review` skill (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/SKILL.md`).
   227	
   228	---
   229	
   230	## 8. EXAMPLES
   231	
   232	```
   233	/spec_kit:deep-research:auto "WebSocket reconnection strategies across browsers"
   234	/spec_kit:deep-research:confirm "distributed caching patterns for microservices"
   235	/spec_kit:deep-research:auto "API rate limiting approaches" --max-iterations 5 --convergence 0.10
   236	```
   237	
   238	---
   239	
   240	## 9. NEXT STEPS
   241	
   242	| Condition | Suggested Command | Reason |
   243	|-----------|-------------------|--------|
   244	| Research complete, ready to plan | `/spec_kit:plan [feature]` | Use findings for spec/plan |
   245	| Need more investigation | `/spec_kit:deep-research [new-topic]` | Another deep research session |
   246	| Need code/spec audit | `/spec_kit:deep-review [target]` | Iterative code review |
   247	| Want to refresh search support | `/memory:save [spec-folder]` | Refresh the indexed support artifact while canonical continuity stays in spec docs |
   248	| Need to pause | `/spec_kit:handover [spec-folder]` | Save context for later |
   249	
   250	---
   251	
   252	## 10. ERROR HANDLING
   253	
   254	| Error | Action |
   255	|-------|--------|
   256	| Agent dispatch timeout | Retry once with reduced scope, then mark timeout |
   257	| State file missing | Reconstruct from iteration files |
   258	| 3+ consecutive failures | Halt loop, enter synthesis with partial findings |
   259	| Memory save failure | Save to `scratch/` as backup |
   260

    11	---
    12	# Tasks: Deep Research and Deep Review Runtime Improvement Bundle
    13	
    14	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->
    15	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core | v2.2 -->
    16	
    17	---
    18	
    19	<!-- ANCHOR:notation -->
    20	## Task Notation
    21	
    22	| Prefix | Meaning |
    23	|--------|---------|
    24	| `[ ]` | Pending |
    25	| `[x]` | Completed |
    26	| `[P]` | Parallelizable |
    27	| `[B]` | Blocked |
    28	
    29	**Task Format**: `T### or phase-specific ID [P?] Description (file path)`
    30	<!-- /ANCHOR:notation -->
    31	
    32	---
    33	
    34	### AI Execution Protocol
    35	
    36	### Pre-Task Checklist
    37	
    38	- Confirm the child phase before editing.
    39	- Confirm the task still belongs in the linked child folder rather than the parent packet.
    40	- Confirm the verification path listed in the child phase packet.
    41	
    42	### Execution Rules
    43	
    44	| Rule | Requirement |
    45	|------|-------------|
    46	| `CHILD-OWNED` | Detailed implementation tasks belong in child phase `tasks.md` files. |
    47	| `PARENT-INDEX-ONLY` | This root file tracks phase-level progress only. |
    48	| `STATUS-TRACEABLE` | Parent status summaries must match the child task files. |
    49	| `DEFERRED-EXPLICIT` | Phase 4b work stays marked deferred/blocked until prerequisites exist. |
    50	
    51	### Status Reporting Format
    52	
    53	- `pending`: no implementation has started in the child phase.
    54	- `in-progress`: active work is happening in the child phase.
    55	- `blocked`: the child phase has explicit prerequisite or verification blockers.
    56	- `completed`: the child phase implementation and validation gates are complete.
    57	
    58	### Blocked Task Protocol
    59	
    60	- If a blocker belongs to one child phase, record it in that child phase rather than widening the parent packet.
    61	- If a later phase is blocked by an earlier phase, preserve the dependency in both the child phase and the parent index.
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	<!-- ANCHOR:phase-1 -->
    66	## Phase 1: Setup
    67	
    68	### Parent Phase Index
    69	
    70	| Phase | Child Tasks | Requirement Scope | Status Summary | Notes |
    71	|-------|-------------|-------------------|----------------|-------|
    72	| **Phase 1** | [./001-runtime-truth-foundation/](./001-runtime-truth-foundation/) | Runtime truth foundation | Implemented; all tasks completed | 44 files, +7K lines, stop contract, legal-stop gates, resume semantics, journals, dashboards, tests |
    73	| **Phase 2** | [./002-semantic-coverage-graph/tasks.md](./002-semantic-coverage-graph/tasks.md) | Coverage graph + MCP reuse | Implemented; 23/23 tasks completed | 25 files (17 new), +5.2K lines, 4 CJS modules, SQLite DB, MCP handlers, 101 tests |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1423:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/README.md:30:`sk-deep-review` runs autonomous, multi-iteration code quality audits on targets that require more than a single review pass to assess thoroughly. Each iteration dispatches a fresh `@deep-review` agent with no prior context, using file-based state (JSONL log, strategy file, iteration notes) to carry findings forward without degrading the context window. When new findings drop below a configurable threshold across multiple consecutive iterations, a 3-signal composite convergence algorithm stops the loop and triggers final synthesis, producing a `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md` with a binary release readiness verdict.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1480:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:110:8. **Initialize reducer state**: Create `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json` with empty `openFindings`, `resolvedFindings`, `repeatedFindings`, `dimensionCoverage`, `findingsBySeverity`, and `convergenceScore`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1481:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:147:- Read `deep-review-findings-registry.json` -- extract `dimensionsCovered`, `findingsBySeverity`, `openFindings`, `resolvedFindings`, and `convergenceScore`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1496:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/023-fail-closed-reducer.md:50:| DRV-023 | Review reducer fails closed on corruption and missing anchors | Verify corruption and missing anchors fail closed by default, with `--lenient` and `--create-missing-anchors` as explicit escape hatches. | Validate fail-closed reducer behavior for sk-deep-review. Confirm that malformed JSONL exits with code `2` unless `--lenient` is passed, that missing machine-owned anchors throw `Missing machine-owned anchor ...` unless `--create-missing-anchors` is used, and that `corruptionWarnings` remains present after lenient recovery, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 2. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 3. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 4. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture} --lenient; echo "exit: $?"` -> 5. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 6. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture} --create-missing-anchors; echo "exit: $?"` -> 7. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {anchor_fixture}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | Without `--lenient`, corrupt JSONL exits `2` and preserves `corruptionWarnings`; without `--create-missing-anchors`, the reducer throws `Missing machine-owned anchor ...`; with `--lenient`, the reducer exits `0` but preserves `corruptionWarnings`; with `--create-missing-anchors`, the missing anchor is created and the reducer proceeds. | Capture both exit codes, the missing-anchor stderr, the populated `.corruptionWarnings` field before and after `--lenient`, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor after `--create-missing-anchors`. | PASS if all four exit conditions match the documented contract and warning state is still visible after lenient recovery; FAIL if any exit code, error string, warning surface, or anchor bootstrap behavior differs. | Privilege `reduce-state.cjs` for exit semantics and `review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` for concrete expected behavior. If fixture preparation accidentally combines both failures in one directory, split into two fixture copies before judging the reducer. |

Confirm that running `node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>` on a review packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event preserves the review gate bundle in `blockedStopHistory`, renders `BLOCKED STOPS` in the dashboard, and rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor with the recovery strategy, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}` -> 2. `bash: cat {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.blockedStopHistory'` -> 3. `bash: grep -A 5 "BLOCKED STOPS" {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-dashboard.md` -> 4. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | `blockedStopHistory` contains reducer-promoted blocked-stop entries with the review gate names; `BLOCKED STOPS` renders each blocked-stop event; `ANCHOR:next-focus` includes the recovery strategy from the latest blocked-stop record. | Capture the populated `blockedStopHistory` array, the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` excerpt, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor showing the recovery guidance. | PASS if all three review surfaces show the same blocked-stop data and preserve the review gate names; FAIL if any surface is missing blocked-stop data or the gate bundle is incomplete after the reducer run. | Privilege `deep-review-findings-registry.json` as the reducer-owned source of truth. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1456:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:50:      "file": ".opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1457:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:52:      "description": "`coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1458:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:232:      "description": "The REQ-024 suite only inserts disjoint node and edge IDs per session (`q-a`/`q-b`, `a-answers-1`/`b-answers-1`) across `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:62-91`, so it proves filtered reads on non-overlapping fixtures but never exercises the global-ID overwrite path in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`. The test can stay green while the runtime still corrupts session isolation under shared IDs.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1459:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:292:      "description": "The summary says `session-isolation.vitest.ts` validates that concurrent sessions cannot see each other's graph nodes, but the shipped suite only seeds disjoint node and edge IDs (`q-a`/`q-b`, `a-answers-1`/`b-answers-1`) and asserts filtered reads on those non-overlapping fixtures at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91`. The underlying storage layer still upserts by bare `id` only at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`, so phase 008 only proved session-scoped reads, not shared-ID collision isolation. The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1460:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-state.jsonl:2:{"type":"iteration","mode":"review","run":1,"status":"complete","focus":"correctness contracts on review loop runtime","dimensions":["correctness","traceability"],"filesReviewed":[".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-strategy.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md",".opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs",".opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs",".opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml",".opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml",".opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml",".opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts",".opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts",".opencode/command/spec_kit/deep-review.md",".opencode/agent/deep-review.md"],"findingsCount":3,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":2,"P2":1},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":2,"P2":1},"newFindingsRatio":1,"sessionId":"rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-11T13:57:15Z","durationMs":429000}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1490:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:21:The dominant pattern across the 10 iterations was contract drift, not isolated code polish. The bundle added meaningful reducer, workflow, and test hardening, but several user-visible promises still do not match the live execution path: STOP gating does not consume claim-adjudication state, lifecycle branches are advertised without persisted lineage transitions, and the coverage-graph namespace contract is still weak at both storage and documentation layers.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2922:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:292:      "description": "The summary says `session-isolation.vitest.ts` validates that concurrent sessions cannot see each other's graph nodes, but the shipped suite only seeds disjoint node and edge IDs (`q-a`/`q-b`, `a-answers-1`/`b-answers-1`) and asserts filtered reads on those non-overlapping fixtures at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91`. The underlying storage layer still upserts by bare `id` only at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`, so phase 008 only proved session-scoped reads, not shared-ID collision isolation. The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",

The delivered work added an append-only audit journal, an improvement-scoped mutation coverage graph, dimension trade-off detection, candidate lineage tracking for optional parallel waves, and benchmark stability scoring so the improver loop could behave like the deeper research and review loops instead of remaining an isolated evaluator.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:56:Before this phase shipped, the improve-agent loop had strong evaluator logic but weak runtime truth. It lacked a dedicated audit journal, had no improvement-scoped coverage graph, could not detect cross-dimension trade-offs, had no candidate-lineage model for parallel exploration, and could not measure benchmark replay stability. That left the loop harder to audit, less explainable, and less aligned with the runtime-truth contracts already established for `sk-deep-research` and `sk-deep-review`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:95:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` | Created | Improvement-scoped coverage graph and exhausted-mutation tracking. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:150:3. **Given** a reader needs the architectural rationale, **when** they open `decision-record.md`, **then** they can see why journal writes stayed in the orchestrator, why coverage graph reuse was chosen, and how backward compatibility was protected.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:118:| REQ-002 | Research and review must actively consult the coverage graph during live stop evaluation. | Graph upsert and graph convergence calls are documented in the shipped research and review releases. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:142:- **SC-002**: The packet shows that the coverage graph is actively consulted on the live research and review loop paths.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/description.json:3:  \\\"description\\\": \\\"Phase 8: Further deep-loop improvements — land 12 P0/P1/P2 recommendations surfaced by the 20-iteration deep-research audit across sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, sk-improve-agent, and the shared coverage graph. put the coverage graph onto the live research and review YAML paths: `deep_loop_graph_upsert` is present after iteration reduction and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` is present before the inline stop vote in both loop families. The remaining release-level gaps are in contract fidelity around what gets persisted and surfaced: the reducers do not currently expose a trustworthy `graphConvergenceScore`, review-side blocked-stop payloads can serialize blocker objects into a `string[]` contract, session scoping is still optional/incomplete in shared read helpers, and review `next-focus` can stay pinned to an old blocked stop after later iterations.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/scratch/closing-review.md:85:  - REQ-012 and ADR-001/003 treat `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` scoping as mandatory, but the handlers still intentionally fall back to `all_sessions_default` aggregation when `sessionId` is omitted. More importantly, `findProvenanceChain()` delegates to helpers that filter by `sessionId` only and do not constrain by `specFolder` or `loopType`, so provenance reads are not fully namespaced even when a namespace object is already available.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/scratch/closing-review.md:88:  - Remove the cross-session default from the phase-008 coverage-graph read surfaces, make `sessionId` mandatory on these handlers/helpers, and thread `specFolder` + `loopType` through the provenance helper queries as well.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/scratch/closing-review.md:109:| REQ-012 | **Gap** | Session scoping is still optional and incomplete. all listed research relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; research metrics use the defined formulas. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:342:| REQ-004 | Review coverage graphs MUST use the defined node kinds, relation types, initial weight estimates, and metadata contract. | `DIMENSION`, `FILE`, `FINDING`, `EVIDENCE`, and `REMEDIATION` are canonical; all listed review relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; review metrics use the defined formulas. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:353:| REQ-010 | Human-readable graph status summaries MUST be part of the contract, not a debugging afterthought. | `deep_loop_graph_status` returns documented counts and signal summaries suitable for dashboards and synthesis surfaces; finding'));\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5551:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-cross-layer.vitest.ts:378:      sessionId,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5574:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5581:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5584:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5586:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5588:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5596:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5600:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5641:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5647:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5681:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5688:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5691:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5693:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5695:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5703:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5707:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5748:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5754:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:3:description: \\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:71:1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:72:2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:75:5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:114:| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:90:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does the coverage graph's contribution to the 3-signal convergence math exceed its weight, or is it merely nominal?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:301:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`sk-improve-agent` follows a different path entirely: its resume contract replays a local \\\\\\\"coverage graph + registry\\\\\\\", and `mutation-coverage.cjs` persists an `improvement` JSON graph with `fs`/`path` helpers and `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the agent only consumes an orchestrator-provided summary as advisory context. That means it does not supply the missing shared `deep_loop_graph_*` bridge for research/review convergence (.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:9-10; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:50-67; .opencode/agent/agent-improver.md:175-177).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:319:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"Both deep-loop convergence contracts still document a reducer-owned in-memory graph bridge that is not present in the live reducer: deep research says \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph\\\\\\\" when `graphEvents` exist, and deep review says the same for review iterations (.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205; .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:658-683).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:711:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published deep-research contract says that when `graphEvents` exist, \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph and derives additional signals for the legal-stop gate evaluation,\\\\\\\" including graph coverage sub-checks (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205`). The live research reducer does not implement that bridge: its only imports are `fs` and `path`, and its convergence score is taken from `latestIteration.convergenceSignals?.compositeStop` or `latestIteration?.newInfoRatio` rather than any graph helper (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-13`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:244-249`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1530:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2121:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, memory-platform redesign work from other research bundles unless it is directly required by the scoped runtime improvements in this packet.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7:- The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:10:- The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6269:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6320:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 into the sk-improve-agent skill across four sub-phases. Sub-phase 5a delivers the core runtime contracts (stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journal, hypothesis ledger). Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. All new config fields are optional with defaults, preserving backward compatibility.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:650:- [ ] 042 Phase 2 coverage graph API confirmed to support `loop_type` namespace parameter\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:708:Add mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, and exhausted-mutations logging. When Opus receives a complex prompt with `--effort high`, it generates an internal chain-of-thought before responding. This matters for architecture trade-off analysis, root cause debugging, and multi-dimensional decision-making where surface-level pattern matching falls short. The reasoning is not just longer responses. It is a fundamentally different processing mode that considers alternatives, backtracks, and synthesizes before committing to an answer.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2410:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:**Context**: Phase 2 built a coverage graph in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4704:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. Open question: how should unresolved nodes from retired segments contribute to the final global stop-decision trace?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4720:- Normalize merge on explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, `run`, `findingId`. Never trust append order.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4752:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. Open question: how should unresolved nodes from retired segments contribute to the final global stop-decision trace?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4768:- Normalize merge on explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, `run`, `findingId`. Never trust append order.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:19:- **F004**: Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154` — `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:27:| spec_code | fail | hard | `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99` | REQ-012 requires shared graph helpers to scope by `specFolder + loopType + sessionId`, but the underlying write identity is still global by bare `id`. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The `all_sessions_default` path is explicitly documented bootstrap/debug behavior rather than a hidden bypass — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:70`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:174`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:3:description: \\\"Deliver the deep-loop coverage graph substrate by extracting shared graph primitives, adding dedicated storage and MCP tools, and wiring graph-backed convergence into deep research and deep review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:7:  -
