were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1379:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:55:- v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation using the Phase 002 coverage graph once the graph surfaces are operational.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1380:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:87:- Add v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation that refines decomposition using the Phase 002 coverage graph once it is operational.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1384:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:153:| REQ-005 | Each segment MUST use Phase 002 graph signals independently for convergence, pruning, promotion, and v2 decomposition upgrades. | Segment convergence explicitly references coverage-graph status or convergence outputs; prune logic can retire converged or stuck segments without dropping unresolved global gaps silently; graph-enhanced segmentation is blocked until the Phase 002 coverage graph is operational. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1393:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/plan.md:264:| Phase 3b graph-enhanced segmentation | Medium | Depends on Phase 002 coverage graph being operational and stable enough to refine v1 safely. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1405:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:19:- **F004**: Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154` — `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1408:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The `all_sessions_default` path is explicitly documented bootstrap/debug behavior rather than a hidden bypass — `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:70`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:174`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1409:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:37:6. Convergence per segment: Each segment needs its own convergence assessment. Should each segment have its own coverage graph instance? Or one graph with segment-scoped queries?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1424:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4704:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agent/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. Open question: how should unresolved nodes from retired segments contribute to the final global stop-decision trace?\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1425:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4752:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. 2 | security session scoping on coverage graph runtime | security, traceability, maintainability | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0.40 | complete |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1666:   153\\t| REQ-005 | Each segment MUST use Phase 002 graph signals converged or stuck segments without dropping unresolved global gaps silently; graph-enhanced segmentation is blocked until the Phase 002 coverage graph is operational. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1758:     3\\tdescription: \\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1815:    60\\tPhases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1826:    71\\t1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1827:    72\\t2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1830:    75\\t5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1869:   114\\t| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 protocol still shows `{\\\"type\\\":\\\"event\\\",\\\"event\\\":\\\"resumed\\\",\\\"fromIteration\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2881:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-007.md:42:-  lineage_mode: \\\"[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2981:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:166:          log: \\\"Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3245:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1489:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:16:- **Scope**: Entire 042 bundle — `spec.md` plus all 8 phase folders (`001` through `008`) and shipped runtime code across `sk-deep-research`, `sk-deep-review`, `sk-improve-agent`, the `system-spec-kit` coverage-graph stack, 6 YAML workflows, vitest suites, changelog surfaces, and packet-root completion artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3246:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1490:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:21:The dominant pattern across the 10 iterations was contract drift, not isolated code polish. The bundle added meaningful reducer, workflow, and test hardening, but several user-visible promises still do not match the live execution path: STOP gating does not consume claim-adjudication state, lifecycle branches are advertised without persisted lineage transitions, and the coverage-graph namespace contract is still weak at both storage and documentation layers.

The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",

But the current review session still carries unresolved required findings on claim-adjudication contract drift and improve-agent lineage execution claims at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:18` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:19`, and the reducer-generated dashboard still reports `P1 (Required) | 8` with a next focus explicitly aimed at those remaining gaps at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:35` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:95`. That makes the bundle’s primary closeout summary materially ahead of the evidence now available on release readiness.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3467:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-009.md:22:- **F015**: Reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` summary hides non-P0 release-readiness debt — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:832` — `renderDashboard()` only emits an active-risk warning when the latest iteration errored or when `severity.P0 > 0`; otherwise it hardcodes `None active beyond normal review uncertainty.` The generated dashboard therefore says there are no active risks at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:100` even though the same dashboard reports eight active P1 findings and thirteen open findings at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:35` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:82`. For a release-readiness dashboard, that summary is cleaner than the underlying state and makes quick scans easier to misread.

For a release-readiness dashboard, that summary is cleaner than the underlying state and makes quick scans easier to misread.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3756:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2865:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-009.md:22:- **F015**: Reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` summary hides non-P0 release-readiness debt — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:832` — `renderDashboard()` only emits an active-risk warning when the latest iteration errored or when `severity.P0 > 0`; otherwise it hardcodes `None active beyond normal review uncertainty.` The generated dashboard therefore says there are no active risks at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:100` even though the same dashboard reports eight active P1 findings and thirteen open findings at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:35` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:82`. For a release-readiness dashboard, that summary is cleaner than the underlying state and makes quick scans easier to misread.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3801:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1683:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/tasks.md:80:| **Closing Audit + Remediation** | [./review/review-report.md](./review/review-report.md) | Post-phase-008 closing audit (10 iterations, CONDITIONAL) and Lane 1–5 remediation | Closed — 16 findings routed to 5 remediation lanes, all landed in this packet | See `implementation-summary.md` §How It Was Delivered for the Lane 1–5 mapping and REQ-026 through REQ-034 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3813:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1763:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2890:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:141:- **Required decision**: either implement `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` as real runtime branches with event emission and ancestry metadata or narrow every user-facing promise to match the current one-session model.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3829:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2713:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:72:      "description": "The research state-format reference lists optional iteration fields through `sourceStrength` but never documents `graphEvents`, even though the live research confirm workflow requires `graphEvents` objects shaped as raw `{type, id, label, relation?, source?, target?}` and then preserves those IDs unchanged into `deep_loop_graph_upsert` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:438` and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:464-475`. The review side is only slightly better: it names `graphEvents` as an optional field with no schema or namespace guidance at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:192-194`, while the confirm mirror still emits raw `{type, id, ...}` events and deduplicates/upserts by bare `id` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:597` and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:658-665`. Because the runtime write layer already keys rows by bare ID (see F004), the missing doc-level namespace contract leaves the session-isolation requirement invisible to operators and makes the collision hazard easy to reintroduce.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3830:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2718:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:272:      "description": "The research loop protocol still shows `{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"resumed\",\"fromIteration\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3831:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2719:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:292:      "description": "The summary says `session-isolation.vitest.ts` validates that concurrent sessions cannot see each other's graph nodes, but the shipped suite only seeds disjoint node and edge IDs (`q-a`/`q-b`, `a-answers-1`/`b-answers-1`) and asserts filtered reads on those non-overlapping fixtures at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91`. The underlying storage layer still upserts by bare `id` only at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`, so phase 008 only proved session-scoped reads, not shared-ID collision isolation. The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3832:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2720:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:16:- **Scope**: Entire 042 bundle — `spec.md` plus all 8 phase folders (`001` through `008`) and shipped runtime code across `sk-deep-research`, `sk-deep-review`, `sk-improve-agent`, the `system-spec-kit` coverage-graph stack, 6 YAML workflows, vitest suites, changelog surfaces, and packet-root completion artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3833:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2721:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:18:- **Coverage**: all 4 dimensions reached during the session: correctness, security, traceability, and maintainability
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3834:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2722:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:21:The dominant pattern across the 10 iterations was contract drift, not isolated code polish. The bundle added meaningful reducer, workflow, and test hardening, but several user-visible promises still do not match the live execution path: STOP gating does not consume claim-adjudication state, lifecycle branches are advertised without persisted lineage transitions, and the coverage-graph namespace contract is still weak at both storage and documentation layers.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3835:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2723:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:36:| F004 | P1 | security | Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide | `I002` (`iteration-002.md:19`) | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` | 2 | 2 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3836:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2724:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:37:| F005 | P2 | security | Session-isolation regression omits the ID-collision path | `I002` (`iteration-002.md:22`) | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:62`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376` | 2 | 2 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3837:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2725:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:38:| F006 | P1 | security | Graph-event namespace contract is still undocumented on the visible path | `I003` (`iteration-003.md:19`) | `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:145`; `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:658-665` | 3 | 3 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3838:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2726:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:45:| F013 | P2 | traceability | Phase 008 implementation summary overclaims REQ-024 closure | `I008` (`iteration-008.md:19`) | `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md:59`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91` | 8 | 8 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3839:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2727:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:65:- **Risk**: the coverage graph still mutates rows by bare ID, so two sessions reusing the same node or edge ID can overwrite each other.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3840:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2728:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:66:- **Carry-forward note**: this is the core session-isolation defect; the other graph findings are downstream evidence, tests, and docs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3841:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2729:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:73:- **Risk**: visible review and research references do not tell operators how graph-event IDs must remain namespaced or unique per session.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3842:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2730:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:130:- **Why second**: this is the only active security lane, and every release-facing REQ-024 claim depends on the storage contract actually being session-safe under collision.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3843:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2731:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:131:- **Target surfaces**: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md`, phase 008 closeout docs
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3844:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2732:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:132:- **Required decision**: choose a stable storage namespace model such as composite primary keys or deterministic session-qualified IDs; then document that model on the visible graph-event contract.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3845:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2733:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:133:- **Exit condition**: shared-ID collisions no longer overwrite prior session rows, the isolation test fails on the pre-fix path and passes on the repaired path, and REQ-024 closeout language cites the repaired test coverage honestly.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3846:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2734:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:141:- **Required decision**: either implement `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` as real runtime branches with event emission and ancestry metadata or narrow every user-facing promise to match the current one-session model.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3847:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2735:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:178:1. **REQ-026 [correctness] Claim adjudication must participate in legal STOP eligibility.**

The follow-up remediation packet should therefore treat every closeout artifact as downstream of the runtime contract, not as independent evidence.

The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3990:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:923:    "review-report.md": "mutable",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3991:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1423:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/README.md:30:`sk-deep-review` runs autonomous, multi-iteration code quality audits on targets that require more than a single review pass to assess thoroughly. Each iteration dispatches a fresh `@deep-review` agent with no prior context, using file-based state (JSONL log, strategy file, iteration notes) to carry findings forward without degrading the context window. When new findings drop below a configurable threshold across multiple consecutive iterations, a 3-signal composite convergence algorithm stops the loop and triggers final synthesis, producing a `{spec_folder}/review/review-report.md` with a binary release readiness verdict.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3993:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1527:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/06--synthesis-save-and-guardrails/028-finding-deduplication-and-registry.md:28:- Orchestrator prompt: Validate the finding deduplication contract for sk-deep-review. Confirm that during synthesis, findings from all `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` files are compared for duplicates, that duplicate resolution uses adjudicated `finalSeverity` (taking the highest severity when the same finding appears at different levels), that the Active Finding Registry in `review-report.md` contains only unique findings with their final severity and evidence, and that deduplication does not discard P0 findings, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:3997:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:2439:   612	## 7. Confirm that during synthesis, findings from all `review/iterations/iteration-NNN.md` files are compared for duplicates, that duplicate resolution uses adjudicated `finalSeverity` (taking the highest severity when the same finding appears at different levels), that the Active Finding Registry in `review-report.md` contains only unique findings with their final severity and evidence, and that deduplication does not discard P0 findings, then return a concise operator-facing verdict.

The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4043:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1489:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:16:- **Scope**: Entire 042 bundle — `spec.md` plus all 8 phase folders (`001` through `008`) and shipped runtime code across `sk-deep-research`, `sk-deep-review`, `sk-improve-agent`, the `system-spec-kit` coverage-graph stack, 6 YAML workflows, vitest suites, changelog surfaces, and packet-root completion artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4044:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1490:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:21:The dominant pattern across the 10 iterations was contract drift, not isolated code polish. The bundle added meaningful reducer, workflow, and test hardening, but several user-visible promises still do not match the live execution path: STOP gating does not consume claim-adjudication state, lifecycle branches are advertised without persisted lineage transitions, and the coverage-graph namespace contract is still weak at both storage and documentation layers.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4045:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1491:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:36:| F004 | P1 | security | Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide | `I002` (`iteration-002.md:19`) | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` | 2 | 2 | active |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4047:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1493:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:65:- **Risk**: the coverage graph still mutates rows by bare ID, so two sessions reusing the same node or edge ID can overwrite each other.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4059:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1869:   114	| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4064:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1683:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/tasks.md:80:| **Closing Audit + Remediation** | [./review/review-report.md](./review/review-report.md) | Post-phase-008 closing audit (10 iterations, CONDITIONAL) and Lane 1–5 remediation | Closed — 16 findings routed to 5 remediation lanes, all landed in this packet | See `implementation-summary.md` §How It Was Delivered for the Lane 1–5 mapping and REQ-026 through REQ-034 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4076:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1763:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2890:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:141:- **Required decision**: either implement `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` as real runtime branches with event emission and ancestry metadata or narrow every user-facing promise to match the current one-session model.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4092:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2713:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:72:      "description": "The research state-format reference lists optional iteration fields through `sourceStrength` but never documents `graphEvents`, even though the live research confirm workflow requires `graphEvents` objects shaped as raw `{type, id, label, relation?, source?, target?}` and then preserves those IDs unchanged into `deep_loop_graph_upsert` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:438` and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:464-475`. The review side is only slightly better: it names `graphEvents` as an optional field with no schema or namespace guidance at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:192-194`, while the confirm mirror still emits raw `{type, id, ...}` events and deduplicates/upserts by bare `id` at `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:597` and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:658-665`. Because the runtime write layer already keys rows by bare ID (see F004), the missing doc-level namespace contract leaves the session-isolation requirement invisible to operators and makes the collision hazard easy to reintroduce.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4093:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2718:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:272:      "description": "The research loop protocol still shows `{\"type\":\"event\",\"event\":\"resumed\",\"fromIteration\":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4094:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2719:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:292:      "description": "The summary says `session-isolation.vitest.ts` validates that concurrent sessions cannot see each other's graph nodes, but the shipped suite only seeds disjoint node and edge IDs (`q-a`/`q-b`, `a-answers-1`/`b-answers-1`) and asserts filtered reads on those non-overlapping fixtures at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91`. The underlying storage layer still upserts by bare `id` only at `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` and `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`, so phase 008 only proved session-scoped reads, not shared-ID collision isolation. The implementation summary therefore reports REQ-024 as fully closed on evidence that does not cover the active collision hazard already captured by F004/F005.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4095:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2720:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:16:- **Scope**: Entire 042 bundle — `spec.md` plus all 8 phase folders (`001` through `008`) and shipped runtime code across `sk-deep-research`, `sk-deep-review`, `sk-improve-agent`, the `system-spec-kit` coverage-graph stack, 6 YAML workflows, vitest suites, changelog surfaces, and packet-root completion artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4096:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2721:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:18:- **Coverage**: all 4 dimensions reached during the session: correctness, security, traceability, and maintainability
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4097:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2722:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:21:The dominant pattern across the 10 iterations was contract drift, not isolated code polish. The bundle added meaningful reducer, workflow, and test hardening, but several user-visible promises still do not match the live execution path: STOP gating does not consume claim-adjudication state, lifecycle branches are advertised without persisted lineage transitions, and the coverage-graph namespace contract is still weak at both storage and documentation layers.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4098:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2723:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:36:| F004 | P1 | security | Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide | `I002` (`iteration-002.md:19`) | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302` | 2 | 2 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4099:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2724:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:37:| F005 | P2 | security | Session-isolation regression omits the ID-collision path | `I002` (`iteration-002.md:22`) | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:62`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376` | 2 | 2 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4100:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2725:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:38:| F006 | P1 | security | Graph-event namespace contract is still undocumented on the visible path | `I003` (`iteration-003.md:19`) | `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:145`; `.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:658-665` | 3 | 3 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4101:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2726:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:45:| F013 | P2 | traceability | Phase 008 implementation summary overclaims REQ-024 closure | `I008` (`iteration-008.md:19`) | `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md:59`; `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts:61-91` | 8 | 8 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4102:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2727:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:65:- **Risk**: the coverage graph still mutates rows by bare ID, so two sessions reusing the same node or edge ID can overwrite each other.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4103:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2728:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:66:- **Carry-forward note**: this is the core session-isolation defect; the other graph findings are downstream evidence, tests, and docs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4104:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2729:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:73:- **Risk**: visible review and research references do not tell operators how graph-event IDs must remain namespaced or unique per session.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4105:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2730:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:130:- **Why second**: this is the only active security lane, and every release-facing REQ-024 claim depends on the storage contract actually being session-safe under collision.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4106:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2731:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:131:- **Target surfaces**: `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/session-isolation.vitest.ts`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md`, phase 008 closeout docs
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4107:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2732:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:132:- **Required decision**: choose a stable storage namespace model such as composite primary keys or deterministic session-qualified IDs; then document that model on the visible graph-event contract.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4108:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2733:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:133:- **Exit condition**: shared-ID collisions no longer overwrite prior session rows, the isolation test fails on the pre-fix path and passes on the repaired path, and REQ-024 closeout language cites the repaired test coverage honestly.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4109:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2734:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:141:- **Required decision**: either implement `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` as real runtime branches with event emission and ancestry metadata or narrow every user-facing promise to match the current one-session model.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4110:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2735:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:178:1. **REQ-026 [correctness] Claim adjudication must participate in legal STOP eligibility.**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4111:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2736:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:181:2. **REQ-027 [traceability] Claim-adjudication packet schema must be canonical on the visible path.**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-015.md:4112:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2737:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:184:3. **REQ-028 [security] Coverage-graph storage identity must be session-isolated under shared IDs.**

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1131:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9:- `sk-deep-review` also stays inside declared reducer surfaces during normal refresh: `reduceReviewState()` writes only `deep-review-findings-registry.json`, `deep-review-strategy.md`, and `deep-review-dashboard.md`, while the state-format contract separately classifies `review-report.md` as a synthesis-time mutable artifact and the dashboard as fully auto-generated/read-only (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:614-645`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:20-25`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:461-479`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1220:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1496:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/023-fail-closed-reducer.md:50:| DRV-023 | Review reducer fails closed on corruption and missing anchors | Verify corruption and missing anchors fail closed by default, with `--lenient` and `--create-missing-anchors` as explicit escape hatches. | Validate fail-closed reducer behavior for sk-deep-review. Confirm that malformed JSONL exits with code `2` unless `--lenient` is passed, that missing machine-owned anchors throw `Missing machine-owned anchor ...` unless `--create-missing-anchors` is used, and that `corruptionWarnings` remains present after lenient recovery, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 2. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 3. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 4. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture} --lenient; echo "exit: $?"` -> 5. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 6. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture} --create-missing-anchors; echo "exit: $?"` -> 7. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {anchor_fixture}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | Without `--lenient`, corrupt JSONL exits `2` and preserves `corruptionWarnings`; without `--create-missing-anchors`, the reducer throws `Missing machine-owned anchor ...`; with `--lenient`, the reducer exits `0` but preserves `corruptionWarnings`; with `--create-missing-anchors`, the missing anchor is created and the reducer proceeds. | Capture both exit codes, the missing-anchor stderr, the populated `.corruptionWarnings` field before and after `--lenient`, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor after `--create-missing-anchors`. | PASS if all four exit conditions match the documented contract and warning state is still visible after lenient recovery; FAIL if any exit code, error string, warning surface, or anchor bootstrap behavior differs. | Privilege `reduce-state.cjs` for exit semantics and `review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` for concrete expected behavior. If fixture preparation accidentally combines both failures in one directory, split into two fixture copies before judging the reducer. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1225:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:1511:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/022-blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:50:| DRV-022 | Review reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus | Verify review `blocked_stop` output appears in `blockedStopHistory`, `BLOCKED STOPS`, and the strategy recovery anchor. | Validate blocked-stop reducer surfacing for sk-deep-review. Confirm that running `node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>` on a review packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event preserves the review gate bundle in `blockedStopHistory`, renders `BLOCKED STOPS` in the dashboard, and rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor with the recovery strategy, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}` -> 2. `bash: cat {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.blockedStopHistory'` -> 3. `bash: grep -A 5 "BLOCKED STOPS" {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-dashboard.md` -> 4. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | `blockedStopHistory` contains reducer-promoted blocked-stop entries with the review gate names; `BLOCKED STOPS` renders each blocked-stop event; `ANCHOR:next-focus` includes the recovery strategy from the latest blocked-stop record. | Capture the populated `blockedStopHistory` array, the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` excerpt, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor showing the recovery guidance. | PASS if all three review surfaces show the same blocked-stop data and preserve the review gate names; FAIL if any surface is missing blocked-stop data or the gate bundle is incomplete after the reducer run. | Privilege `deep-review-findings-registry.json` as the reducer-owned source of truth. If the registry is correct but the dashboard or strategy anchor is stale, treat that as reducer surfacing drift rather than JSONL input failure. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1234:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:2272:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/022-blocked-stop-reducer-surfacing.md:50:| DRV-022 | Review reducer surfaces blocked-stop history across registry, dashboard, and next-focus | Verify review `blocked_stop` output appears in `blockedStopHistory`, `BLOCKED STOPS`, and the strategy recovery anchor. | Validate blocked-stop reducer surfacing for sk-deep-review. Confirm that running `node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder>` on a review packet with at least one `blocked_stop` event preserves the review gate bundle in `blockedStopHistory`, renders `BLOCKED STOPS` in the dashboard, and rewrites the strategy `next-focus` anchor with the recovery strategy, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}` -> 2. `bash: cat {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.blockedStopHistory'` -> 3. `bash: grep -A 5 "BLOCKED STOPS" {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-dashboard.md` -> 4. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {spec_folder}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | `blockedStopHistory` contains reducer-promoted blocked-stop entries with the review gate names; `BLOCKED STOPS` renders each blocked-stop event; `ANCHOR:next-focus` includes the recovery strategy from the latest blocked-stop record. | Capture the populated `blockedStopHistory` array, the dashboard `BLOCKED STOPS` excerpt, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor showing the recovery guidance. | PASS if all three review surfaces show the same blocked-stop data and preserve the review gate names; FAIL if any surface is missing blocked-stop data or the gate bundle is incomplete after the reducer run. | Privilege `deep-review-findings-registry.json` as the reducer-owned source of truth. If the registry is correct but the dashboard or strategy anchor is stale, treat that as reducer surfacing drift rather than JSONL input failure. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1236:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-013.md:2275:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-and-recovery/023-fail-closed-reducer.md:50:| DRV-023 | Review reducer fails closed on corruption and missing anchors | Verify corruption and missing anchors fail closed by default, with `--lenient` and `--create-missing-anchors` as explicit escape hatches. | Validate fail-closed reducer behavior for sk-deep-review. Confirm that malformed JSONL exits with code `2` unless `--lenient` is passed, that missing machine-owned anchors throw `Missing machine-owned anchor ...` unless `--create-missing-anchors` is used, and that `corruptionWarnings` remains present after lenient recovery, then return a concise operator-facing verdict. | 1. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 2. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 3. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture}; echo "exit: $?"` -> 4. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {corrupt_fixture} --lenient; echo "exit: $?"` -> 5. `bash: cat {corrupt_fixture}/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json | jq '.corruptionWarnings'` -> 6. `bash: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {anchor_fixture} --create-missing-anchors; echo "exit: $?"` -> 7. `bash: sed -n '/ANCHOR:next-focus/,/\/ANCHOR:next-focus/p' {anchor_fixture}/review/deep-review-strategy.md` | Without `--lenient`, corrupt JSONL exits `2` and preserves `corruptionWarnings`; without `--create-missing-anchors`, the reducer throws `Missing machine-owned anchor ...`; with `--lenient`, the reducer exits `0` but preserves `corruptionWarnings`; with `--create-missing-anchors`, the missing anchor is created and the reducer proceeds. | Capture both exit codes, the missing-anchor stderr, the populated `.corruptionWarnings` field before and after `--lenient`, and the strategy `next-focus` anchor after `--create-missing-anchors`. | PASS if all four exit conditions match the documented contract and warning state is still visible after lenient recovery; FAIL if any exit code, error string, warning surface, or anchor bootstrap behavior differs. | Privilege `reduce-state.cjs` for exit semantics and `review-reducer-fail-closed.vitest.ts` for concrete expected behavior. If fixture preparation accidentally combines both failures in one directory, split into two fixture copies before judging the reducer. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:1985:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1351:      "text": "`sk-deep-review` also stays inside declared reducer surfaces during normal refresh: `reduceReviewState()` writes only `deep-review-findings-registry.json`, `deep-review-strategy.md`, and `deep-review-dashboard.md`, while the state-format contract separately classifies `review-report.md` as a synthesis-time mutable artifact and the dashboard as fully auto-generated/read-only (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:614-645`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:20-25`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:461-479`).",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2171:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-009.md:22:- **F015**: Reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` summary hides non-P0 release-readiness debt — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:832` — `renderDashboard()` only emits an active-risk warning when the latest iteration errored or when `severity.P0 > 0`; otherwise it hardcodes `None active beyond normal review uncertainty.` The generated dashboard therefore says there are no active risks at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:100` even though the same dashboard reports eight active P1 findings and thirteen open findings at `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:35` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:82`. For a release-readiness dashboard, that summary is cleaner than the underlying state and makes quick scans easier to misread.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2179:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-state.jsonl:10:{"type":"iteration","mode":"review","run":9,"status":"complete","focus":"release-readiness traceability on bundle summaries and reducer risk surfaces","dimensions":["traceability","maintainability"],"filesReviewed":[".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-strategy.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md",".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md",".opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs",".opencode/changelog/13--sk-deep-review/v1.3.0.0.md",".opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md"],"findingsCount":15,"findingsSummary":{"P0":0,"P1":9,"P2":6},"findingsNew":{"P0":0,"P1":1,"P2":1},"newFindingsRatio":0.13,"sessionId":"rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","timestamp":"2026-04-11T14:28:03Z","durationMs":240000}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2180:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:10:      "file": ".opencode/agent/deep-review.md",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2181:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:12:      "description": "The live agent contract tells the LEAF reviewer to write `# Review Iteration [N]: ...` plus findings like `### P0-NNN:` with freeform bullets, but the reducer only extracts `- **FNNN**:` bullets inside `### P0` / `### P1` / `### P2` subsections from `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:137-206`. A run that follows the canonical agent file can therefore surface zero findings into the registry and dashboard even when the markdown contains substantive defects.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2182:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:152:      "description": "The improvement skill says sessions support `new`, `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue`, and that resume replays prior artifacts to compute `continuedFromIteration` before dispatch (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:292-294`); the operator-facing improve command repeats that contract and says `--session-id=<prior-id>` resumes from the saved journal without re-running completed iterations (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`). But the shipped auto/confirm workflows only accept `target_path`, `target_profile`, `scoring_mode`, `spec_folder`, `max_iterations`, and execution mode as inputs (`.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:36-42`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_confirm.yaml:37-43`), and their runtime only emits `session_start`, per-iteration, and `session_end` journal events with no lineage-branch input or transition step (`.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:132-183`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_confirm.yaml:148-217`). The reducer then summarizes only the latest session timestamps plus `stopReason` / `sessionOutcome` and never surfaces session ancestry, lineage mode, or `continuedFromIteration` (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:184-224`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:781-817`). That leaves the improvement bundle advertising safe resume/restart/fork/reopen behavior that operators cannot actually invoke or audit in the live runtime.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2183:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:310:      "file": ".opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2205:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:33:| F001 | P1 | correctness | Canonical deep-review agent still emits an unparseable iteration schema | `I001` (`iteration-001.md:18`) | `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md:147`; `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:137-206` | 1 | 1 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2206:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:47:| F015 | P2 | traceability | Reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` summary hides non-P0 release-readiness debt | `I009` (`iteration-009.md:22`) | `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:832`; `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/deep-review-dashboard.md:100` | 9 | 9 | active |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2207:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:53:- **Risk**: a canonical reviewer following `.opencode/agent/deep-review.md` can produce markdown the reducer does not parse, which drops real findings out of the registry and dashboard.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-016.md:2208:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:81:- **Risk**: the quick reference teaches the wrong weighted convergence vote, which points operators at the wrong tuning levers during manual audits.
