     8	
     9	# Coverage Graph
    10	
    11	## 1. OVERVIEW
    12	
    13	`lib/coverage-graph/` owns the dedicated deep-loop graph store used by research and review loops.
    14	
    15	- `coverage-graph-db.ts` - SQLite schema, namespace handling, and upsert primitives.
    16	- `coverage-graph-query.ts` - uncovered-question, contradiction, provenance, and hot-node queries.
    17	- `coverage-graph-signals.ts` - convergence and graph-health metrics.
    18	
    19	This subsystem is separate from the causal memory graph and is scoped by `specFolder`, `loopType`, and `sessionId`.
    20	
    21	## 2. RELATED
    22	
    23	- `../../handlers/coverage-graph/README.md`
    24	- `../graph/README.md`

---
     1	---
     2	title: "Coverage Graph Handlers"
     3	description: "MCP handlers for deep-loop research and review coverage graphs."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "coverage graph handlers"
     6	  - "deep loop graph"
     7	---
     8	
     9	# Coverage Graph Handlers
    10	
    11	## 1. OVERVIEW
    12	
    13	`handlers/coverage-graph/` exposes the MCP-facing handler layer for the deep-loop coverage graph tools. The handlers validate tool inputs and delegate storage/query work to `lib/coverage-graph/`.
    14	
    15	Current handler modules:
    16	
    17	- `upsert.ts` - validates and upserts nodes and edges.
    18	- `query.ts` - serves uncovered-question, contradiction, provenance, and hot-node queries.
    19	- `status.ts` - reports graph health and convergence metrics.
    20	- `convergence.ts` - computes stop/continue guidance for research and review loops.
    21	- `index.ts` - barrel export for the handler surface.
    22	
    23	## 2. RELATED
    24	
    25	- `../../lib/coverage-graph/README.md`
    26	- `../README.md`

codex
**Findings**

1. `F-020-001` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability`  
File: [coverage-graph-query.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:91)  
Title: Composite-key namespace rules are still enforced ad hoc in query code  
Description: Phase 2 migrated the coverage graph to composite primary keys, but the query layer still hand-writes namespace scoping instead of using a shared join/filter primitive. That leaves the architecture fragile: future queries can easily regress back to bare `id` lookups, and a few already do. This is a maintainability risk because schema evolution now depends on every query author remembering all namespace columns manually.  
Evidence: [coverage-graph-db.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152) explicitly states that every read/write/update/delete must scope bare IDs by `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)`. But `findCoverageGaps()` uses `NOT EXISTS` subqueries keyed only by `e.source_id = n.id` / `e.target_id = n.id` plus optional `session_id` filtering at [coverage-graph-query.ts:96-101](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:96) and [coverage-graph-query.ts:130-135](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:130). `findContradictions()` joins nodes on `s.id = e.source_id` and `t.id = e.target_id` without joining namespace columns at [coverage-graph-query.ts:171-179](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:171). `findUnverifiedClaims()` repeats the same pattern for `RESOLVES` edges at [coverage-graph-query.ts:349-358](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:349).  
Recommendation: Introduce a namespace-safe query helper or shared SQL fragments for node-edge joins and existence checks, then add regression tests that reuse the same logical IDs across spec folders and sessions.

2. `F-020-002` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability`  
File: [coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:10)  
Title: The DB “contract” suite is frozen against a superseded API and taxonomy  
Description: This test file still describes itself as a pre-implementation contract stub and validates a payload shape that no longer matches the shipped coverage-graph database surface. Keeping it in the active test tree makes the verification story misleading: future maintainers see a green suite that appears authoritative, but it no longer guards the real schema or node/relation vocabulary.  
Evidence: The header says the “actual MCP implementation will be wired in Phase 3” at [coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:10-17](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:10). Its payload uses `namespace`, `type`, and `label` at [coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:21-26](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:21), while the live schema requires `specFolder`, `loopType`, `sessionId`, `kind`, and `name` in [tool-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:706). The test still blesses obsolete taxonomies like `question_node`, `finding_node`, `EXTENDS`, `EVIDENCES`, and `REMEDIATES` at [coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:202-235](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts:202), but the runtime accepts the newer `VALID_KINDS` and `VALID_RELATIONS` set in [coverage-graph-db.ts:136-145](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136).  
Recommendation: Remove this file from the active suite, or rewrite it to exercise the real DB contract and current taxonomy. Archived design-contract tests should be clearly moved or renamed so they cannot be mistaken for runtime coverage.

3. `F-020-003` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability`  
File: [coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:10)  
Title: The MCP tool contract suite is also anchored to a deprecated tool surface  
Description: The tool-level tests have the same problem as the DB tests: they encode an earlier design (`namespace`, `signalsOnly`, fixed `maxDepth` default) rather than the current `deep_loop_graph_*` tool surface. That makes MCP surface growth harder to manage because the test suite no longer documents or enforces the actual public contract.  
Evidence: The file still says the handlers “will be implemented in Phase 3” at [coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:10-17](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:10). Its inputs use `namespace`, `nodeId`, `signalsOnly`, and a hard-coded default depth of 5 at [coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:21-33](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:21) and [coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:87-118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-tools.vitest.ts:87). The shipped schemas instead expose `deep_loop_graph_upsert`, `deep_loop_graph_query`, `deep_loop_graph_status`, and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` with `specFolder`, `loopType`, typed query modes, and convergence inputs at [tool-schemas.ts:706-795](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:706).  
Recommendation: Replace this with real handler/schema tests against the current tool surface, or explicitly quarantine it as a historical design fixture outside the active test directory.

4. `F-020-004` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `maintainability`  
File: [coverage-graph-db.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:237)  
Title: Schema upgrade behavior is destructive, but the operator docs do not describe the rebuild path  
Description: The database layer treats v1-to-v2 migration as a destructive drop-and-recreate operation. That may be acceptable for a disposable cache, but it is an operationally meaningful behavior and should be documented where maintainers look first. Right now the coverage-graph READMEs describe ownership and modules, but not schema versioning, replay/rebuild procedure, or data-loss expectations.  
Evidence: The migration comment says live upgrades “drop the v1 tables before creating the v2 schema” because the DB is treated as a dev-only cache at [coverage-graph-db.ts:237-243](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:237). The library README and handler README only describe module responsibilities at [lib/coverage-graph/README.md:11-23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/README.md:11) and [handlers/coverage-graph/README.md:11-25](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/README.md:11), with no rebuild or migration guidance.  
Recommendation: Document the schema-version policy, destructive-upgrade expectation, and the supported replay/recovery path in the coverage-graph operator docs. If the DB is intentionally disposable, say that explicitly and point to the authoritative regeneration workflow.

5. `F-020-005` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `maintainability`  
File: [042 root spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md:23)  
Title: The parent packet is not self-consistent after phases 5-8 and the phase-7 folder rename  
Description: The root packet now mixes old four-phase language with the newer eight-phase/remediation reality, and some of its references no longer resolve to the actual folder structure. That weakens release-readiness because the top-level packet is supposed to be the operator’s authoritative map for the bundle.  
Evidence: The executive summary lists phases `001`-`008` at [spec.md:23-39](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md:23), but the problem/purpose and scope sections still describe “four execution-ready child phases” at [spec.md:66-70](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md:66) and [spec.md:80-107](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md:80). The packet references `007-graph-aware-stop-gate`, but the actual folder present is `007-skill-rename-improve-agent-prompt` in the current directory listing. `tasks.md` also points closing-audit readers to `review/review-report.md` at [tasks.md:80](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/tasks.md:80), but that file is not present in the live review folder.  
Recommendation: Re-sync the root packet’s phase map, scope text, and closing-audit links to the current folder layout before calling the bundle fully release-ready.

**Release Readiness**

Conditional. I did not find a new P0-level architectural blocker in the reviewed maintainability surfaces, and the remediation work clearly improved runtime/test depth. But the bundle still has two release-facing maintainability risks: the coverage-graph read path has not fully absorbed the composite-key migration, and the active test/docs surfaces still contain stale contracts that make the architecture harder to trust and evolve.

**Iteration Summary**

Total findings: 5  
Breakdown: 0 `P0`, 4 `P1`, 1 `P2`

Coverage assessment: about 85% of the requested focus area. I reviewed the parent packet, phase summaries, coverage-graph DB/query/handler/schema surfaces, representative wave and optimizer modules, and the main graph-related test/doc layers. I did not re-audit every one of the ~200 changed files line by line.

Confidence: high

Next priorities: if a follow-on pass is needed, I would validate live upgrade/replay behavior on an actual v1 coverage DB, inventory which tests are authoritative versus archival across the deep-loop stack, and do one focused pass on MCP surface sprawl outside coverage-graph to confirm the same contract-drift pattern is not repeating in newer tools.
